description: Enhanced OCR layer with TrOCR, PaddleOCR, DocTR support and OCR ensemble voting. Adds multiple free OCR engines with confidence-weighted result merging.
steps: Create src/processors/ocr_engines/__init__.py - package init that exports all OCR engine classes.

Create src/processors/ocr_engines/base_ocr.py - BaseOCREngine abstract class with methods: extract_text(image_path) -> Tuple[str, float], is_available() -> bool, get_name() -> str. This is the interface all OCR engines implement.

Create src/processors/ocr_engines/tesseract_ocr.py - wrap the existing OCREngine (src/processors/ocr_engine.py) logic into the new BaseOCREngine interface. Keep all existing preprocessing logic. Mark as always available if pytesseract is importable.

Create src/processors/ocr_engines/trocr_engine.py - TrOCR (Microsoft transformer-based OCR) engine using transformers library. Use model 'microsoft/trocr-base-printed' for printed text and 'microsoft/trocr-base-handwritten' for handwriting. Implement is_available() to check if transformers and torch are importable. Process image by splitting into line segments and running TrOCR on each. Return concatenated text with confidence from model logits.

Create src/processors/ocr_engines/paddle_ocr_engine.py - PaddleOCR engine wrapper. Use PaddleOCR(use_angle_cls=True, lang='en') for English. Implement is_available() to check if paddleocr is importable. Extract text with bounding boxes and confidence scores. Support multi-language via lang parameter.

Create src/processors/ocr_engines/doctr_engine.py - DocTR (Mindee) engine wrapper. Use doctr.models.ocr_predictor(pretrained=True). Implement is_available() to check if doctr is importable. Process full document pages, return structured text with word-level confidences.

Create src/processors/ocr_ensemble.py - OCRensemble orchestrator. Takes a list of BaseOCREngine instances. For each image: (a) run all available engines, (b) collect results as (text, confidence) pairs, (c) implement confidence-weighted merging - if texts are similar (high overlap), pick the highest confidence one; if texts differ significantly, use character-level voting across engines. Configurable: which engines to include, minimum engines required, fallback strategy.

Update src/processors/ocr_engine.py - keep backward compatibility but add a factory function get_ocr_engine(engine_name) that returns the appropriate engine from the new package. Add get_ocr_ensemble(config) that returns a configured OCREnsemble.
Register all OCR engines in the FreeModelRegistry (src/models/model_registry.py) on import/initialization.

Write tests/test_ocr_engines.py - test each OCR engine class with mocked dependencies (mock transformers pipeline, mock paddleocr, mock doctr). Test: (a) is_available() returns False when deps missing, (b) extract_text returns proper tuple, (c) OCREnsemble merges results correctly, (d) ensemble handles engine failures gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: BaseOCREngine interface is defined with extract_text() and is_available()
TrOCR, PaddleOCR, and DocTR engines implement BaseOCREngine
Each engine returns False from is_available() if its dependencies are not installed
OCREnsemble runs multiple engines and merges results with confidence weighting
Existing Tesseract OCR still works via the backward-compatible interface
OCR engines are registered in the FreeModelRegistry
All new tests pass: python -m pytest tests/test_ocr_engines.py -v
Existing tests still pass: python -m pytest tests/ -v
verification: python -c "from src.processors.ocr_engines import TesseractOCR, TrOCREngine, PaddleOCREngine, DocTREngine; print('OK')"
